medications.name: Metoprolol succinate ER
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily in the morning.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily.

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every evening at bedtime.

medications.name: Levothyroxine
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily on an empty stomach, at least 30 minutes before breakfast and at least 4 hours apart from any calcium, iron, or magnesium supplements; do not switch manufacturers without contacting the prescribing physician, as small differences in formulation can change effective dose; recheck TSH 6-8 weeks after any dose or brand change.

medications.name: Sertraline
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily in the morning with food.

medications.name: Albuterol HFA inhaler
medications.sig: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath; shake well before use; rinse mouth after.

medications.name: Fluticasone propionate nasal spray
medications.sig: 1 spray in each nostril once daily during allergy season.

medications.name: Cetirizine
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily as needed for allergies.

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth once daily before breakfast for 8 weeks, then reassess; do not crush or chew.

medications.name: Ferrous sulfate
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every other day with a glass of orange juice or other source of vitamin C to improve absorption; take on an empty stomach if tolerated, otherwise with a small amount of food; avoid taking within 2 hours of antacids, dairy products, coffee, tea, or levothyroxine; expect dark stools, which are harmless; contact the office if constipation becomes severe or if abdominal pain persists for more than a few days.

medications.name: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol)
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth once daily with the largest meal of the day.

medications.name: Magnesium glycinate
medications.sig: Take 2 capsules by mouth at bedtime.

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily — HOLD pending cardiology evaluation per Dr. Raman's note of 2026-03-02.

medications.name: Sumatriptan
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth at onset of migraine; may repeat once after 2 hours; maximum 200 mg in 24 hours; do not use within 24 hours of another triptan or ergot.

medications.name: Ondansetron ODT
medications.sig: Dissolve 1 tablet on tongue every 8 hours as needed for nausea.

medications.name: Melatonin
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth 30 minutes before bedtime as needed for sleep.

medications.name: Norethindrone
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth at the same time every day, with no pill-free interval.

medications.name: Triamcinolone 0.1% cream
medications.sig: Apply a thin layer to affected area twice daily for up to 14 days; avoid face and skin folds.

medications.name: Artificial tears (carboxymethylcellulose 0.5%)
medications.sig: Instill 1-2 drops in each eye up to 4 times daily as needed for dryness.

medications.name: Ibuprofen
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 8 hours as needed for pain, with food; do not exceed 3 tablets in 24 hours; avoid if stomach upset develops.

medications.name: Propranolol
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily — stopped 2026-03-02 when metoprolol was started.

medications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily in the morning.

medications.name: Escitalopram
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily; cross-tapered to sertraline September 2024 due to fatigue.

medications.name: Zolpidem
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth at bedtime as needed for insomnia; do not take unless able to sleep 7-8 hours.

medications.name: Combined oral contraceptive (ethinyl estradiol/norgestimate)
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth daily as directed on pack; discontinued in favor of progestin-only pill given palpitation workup.

medications.name: Pantoprazole
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily before breakfast; switched to omeprazole per formulary.

medications.name: Naproxen
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily with food as needed for pain.

medications.name: Montelukast
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every evening; discontinued due to vivid dreams.

medications.name: Doxycycline hyclate
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth twice daily for 10 days with a full glass of water; remain upright for 30 minutes after each dose; avoid prolonged sun exposure and use sunscreen, as this medication can cause significant photosensitivity; take 2 hours apart from dairy, antacids, iron, and multivitamins.

medications.name: Amoxicillin-clavulanate
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily for 7 days with food.

medications.name: Azithromycin
medications.sig: Take 2 tablets by mouth on day 1, then 1 tablet daily on days 2 through 5.

medications.name: Prednisone taper
medications.sig: Take 20 mg daily for 3 days, then 10 mg daily for 3 days, then 5 mg daily for 3 days, then stop.

medications.name: Nitrofurantoin monohydrate/macrocrystals
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth twice daily for 5 days with food.

medications.name: Fluconazole
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth as a single dose; may repeat in 72 hours if symptoms persist.

medications.name: Oseltamivir
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth twice daily for 5 days.

medications.name: Cyclobenzaprine
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth at bedtime as needed for muscle spasm, for up to 2 weeks; may cause drowsiness — do not drive after taking.

medications.name: Mupirocin 2% ointment
medications.sig: Apply to affected area three times daily for 10 days.

medications.name: Ketorolac
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 6 hours as needed for severe pain; maximum 5 days total; do not combine with other NSAIDs.

medications.name: Cephalexin
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth four times daily for 7 days.

medications.name: Tramadol
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 6 hours as needed for moderate pain; maximum 4 tablets in 24 hours; may cause dizziness or drowsiness; do not combine with alcohol or other sedating medications; taper if used more than 2 weeks.

labs.test: Methylmalonic acid with reflex to homocysteine and intrinsic factor blocking antibody, serum or plasma, quantitative LC-MS/MS

allergies.reaction: Hives and facial swelling within 1 hour of dose (childhood)
Diffuse maculopapular rash on day 3 of therapy
Lip tingling and throat tightness; carries epinephrine auto-injector
Severe nausea and vomiting; reported by patient, never rechallenged
Localized blistering at ECG electrode sites

problems.condition: Paroxysmal palpitations, under evaluation (suspected supraventricular tachycardia)
Suspected obstructive sleep apnea, study pending

wideTable.title: Recent Cardiology-Relevant Encounters and Diagnostics
rows: Urgent care, walk-in (Eastside Clinic)
Dr. Samuel Whitfield, urgent care attending
Racing heart and lightheadedness after climbing one flight of stairs
HR 118 on arrival, declining to 84 over 40 minutes; BP 142/91; SpO2 99% on room air
12-lead ECG (normal sinus rhythm, no ST changes); high-sensitivity troponin x1 (<6 ng/L)
Symptomatic palpitations, resolved in clinic; no acute coronary findings
Referral placed to primary care within 1 week; return precautions given for syncope or chest pain
Patient reported episode felt identical to prior events; rated fear 8/10, symptoms 5/10
Primary care office visit (Lakeview Family Medicine)
Dr. Priya Raman, internal medicine
Follow-up of urgent care visit for recurrent palpitations
BP 138/88; HR 76 regular; BMI 29.4; trace bilateral ankle edema noted
48-hour Holter monitor ordered; basic metabolic panel; TSH; CBC
Probable paroxysmal SVT vs. inappropriate sinus tachycardia; beta-blocker trial initiated
Started metoprolol succinate 25 mg daily; cardiology referral placed; sleep study discussed
Patient using smartwatch ECG; instructed to export and bring recordings to all visits
Ambulatory diagnostics (Holter monitor return and scan)
Reviewed by Dr. Priya Raman with cardiology overread by Dr. Elena Marsh
Device return after 48-hour ambulatory rhythm monitoring
Two symptomatic episodes captured in diary; longest run 7 minutes at 161 bpm
48-hour Holter: narrow-complex tachycardia with abrupt onset/offset during both diary-flagged episodes
Findings consistent with paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia
Expedited cardiology consult requested; patient counseled on vagal maneuvers pending visit
Patient relieved that 'something finally showed up on a test'; anxiety notably reduced
Telehealth visit (Lakeview Family Medicine, video)
Dr. Priya Raman, internal medicine
Medication tolerance check and pre-cardiology review
Home BP log average 131/84 over 14 days; HR average 68; episodes reduced from 3/week to 1/week
None this visit; prior labs reviewed including iron studies showing deficiency
Partial response to metoprolol; iron deficiency anemia may be aggravating tachycardia
Ferrous sulfate adjusted to every-other-day dosing; cardiology visit confirmed for 2026-06-12
Patient reports more confidence climbing stairs but still avoids carrying laundry alone
Outpatient laboratory draw (Eastside Clinic lab)
Standing orders of Dr. Priya Raman; results released to portal same day
Pre-visit laboratory panel ahead of cardiology consultation
Fasting draw completed without complication; patient tolerated well
CBC, CMP, lipid panel, TSH with free T4, iron studies, NT-proBNP, hs-CRP, B12, MMA
Persistent iron deficiency anemia (Hgb 10.9, ferritin 11); lipids above goal; cardiac markers reassuring
All results forwarded to Dr. Elena Marsh, cardiology, for 2026-06-12 consultation
Patient reviewed every result in portal and prepared a written question list for cardiology
